text: Her fingers glided over his skin, feeling no difference between the mismatched portions . “Been through a lot of places,” he said. “Not all of them have the means to reskin synths. Some of the ones that do don’t have the resources to match pigmentation. I don’t notice it much, anymore. It’ll all be replaced again, eventually .” “Are they all from bullets?” She touched another one. It was likely her imagination, but it felt like his skin warmed with the contact . “Bullets, knives, metal rods, rocks. This one,” he placed his hand over hers, guiding it to the spot where a human’s heart was located, “was from a steel beam in a dust storm. It punched through two millimeters to the side of my power cell .” “And you survived through all of that.” She was awed, even as she cringed inwardly at the damage he’d suffered. At the pain he must’ve endured . His other hand slid beneath the blanket. Lara’s heart fluttered as he ran his palm over her thigh, around the back of her knee, and to her calf. It settled over a long, crescent-shaped scar there . “We’ve all survived our own trials,” he said. His fingertip trailed over the slightly raised skin . Her fingers curled against his chest, and she pressed her lips together, focusing on his words, but her body responded to his touch and the heat trailed in its wake . “I was salvaging,” she said, “and I don’t remember if I slipped or tripped, but I landed on a broken beam. Didn’t feel it at first, but there was a sliver as big as a knife stuck in my leg. Then it hurt like a bitch. Tabitha got all the bits of wood out and we cleaned it up as best we could, but I still got sick .” She lifted her knee as he continued to stroke the scar. “Like real sick. I can’t remember much of it, but Tabitha said I almost died. If it weren’t for her, I would have .” “Bots were created to endure,” Ronin said. “Sometimes it seems like humans were created to suffer. I’m more impressed by your survival than by mine .” “You trying to flatter me?” She smirked, and his hand paused . “Flattery isn’t a function I’m familiar with. It implies a level of dishonesty. I’m simply speaking the truth as I perceive it .” “Flattery can be true, if you mean it. Doesn’t hurt to tell a girl something you admire about her. You should try it sometime. Maybe you — ” “ Everything .” “What?” Lara’s throat was suddenly dry . “Everything I’ve come to know about you, I admire .” “Oh.” Once again, he’d rendered her speechless. What could she say to that? “Guess you got flattery down pretty well, for someone who says he’s not familiar with it .” “If you say so, Lara Brooks.” He wrapped his fingers around her hand and squeezed gently. His thumb traced a delicate path from the base of her pinky to her thumb. “How did your sister come by her scar ?” Lara swallowed, chest tight. Guilt plagued her every time she saw that scar, and now that Tabitha was gone … “I gave it to her .” “Sounds like a story to be told .” “I—” Don’t want to talk about it. Can’t talk about it. But that wasn’t true, was it? Tabitha was dead, but Ronin had been right — she lived on so long as Lara kept her memories, kept her love. “Not much of a story, really. She was teaching me how to use a knife, and I was frustrated and ready to give up. She came up behind me, I think to correct my grip, but I jerked away, insisting I could do it myself. I didn’t know I cut her until I saw the blood .” Her fingers twitched against his skin. “I was a lot more careful from then on, but she never held it against me .” “She must’ve been an amazing woman .” “ She was .” “You’ve learned a lot from her. She would be proud of you .” “There you go, with the flattery again.” She looked away so he wouldn’t see how his words affected her, and though she wanted to brush them off, they sank deep into her chest. Tabitha was gone, but…things might still be okay. Whatever Lara thought about herself, Tabitha had always shown faith in her . “ Lara ?” “ Hmm ?” His hand retraced its original path, sliding back up her calf, stopping high on her leg. He brushed his thumb over the sensitive flesh on the inside of her thigh. Holding her hand captive against his chest, he leaned forward. She turned her head to him slowly, finding his face only inches from hers . “I want you .” Her eyes widened and her mouth opened in a circle, but she couldn’t respond. Those three words, so small, so simple, made her breath ragged and her blood hot. She recalled the explosion of sensation he’d awoken in her. After the crushing news he’d delivered, she needed a distraction, but he was so much more than that . “I want you, too,” she said, and he kissed her before the last word was fully past of her lips . Placing a hand on her back, he eased her down onto the bed. The blanket rasped over her sensitive nipples as he pulled it away. They beaded in the cool air. She raised her knees to cradle his hips as he moved over her, arching her back to press her breast into his palm . She wasn’t running from her pain, this time. Lara wanted to feel what only he could make her feel. Alive . Free . She wanted him . * * * S parks pulsed between the electrodes under Ronin’s skin, lighting all circuits with a near-overwhelming hum.
